1.description: The importance of mortgage companies in the U.S. mortgage market This figure shows the importance of mortgage companies in the U.S. mortgage market during our sample period of 2014 to 2019. The top plot presents the total mortgage origination volume across all loan types by mortgage companies (black line) and depository institutions (dashed line). The bottom plot presents mortgage companies’ share of the market based on loan volume (blue line) and number of loan officers (dashed line).

2.description: Illustration of the infra-marginality problem This figure illustrates the infra-marginality problem in our context through three examples. The examples each plot hypothetical distributions of male and female loan officers’ managerial ability, draw promotion thresholds θ(θ) above which loan officers are promoted to manager, and consider the average ability π(π) of promoted managers. Male loan officers’ managerial ability is distributed standard normal in each example, whereas the female distribution varies. The examples all include gender bias in promotion decisions (⁠θθf=1.1⁠, whereas θθm=1⁠). Panel A shows that if female managerial ability follows an identical standard normal distribution as its male counterpart, then gender bias can be detected in average outcomes since πππf>πm⁠. Panel B shows that if the female distribution of managerial ability is shifted left, then the gender bias can be obscured in average outcomes (note that πππf=πm⁠). Panel C shows that even if the female distribution is centered at zero, but is narrower, then the bias is again obscured in average outcomes. See Section 4.2 for additional discussion.
